name: Riad's Succulent White Beans
recipeIngredient: 3 plum tomatoes, ripe, seeds removed, quartered
recipeInstructions:
1. Rinse the beans and soak in tepid water overnight.
2. Rinse and drain the beans several times in cold water.
3. Place the beans in a large, heavy pot. Add the 8 cups of water and bay leaves. Bring to a boil. Reduce heat to medium and simmer until the beans are tender but not mushy, about 40 to 45 minutes, skimming any foam that rises to the surface. Drain the beans (discarding bay leaves) and set aside.
4. In another heavy pot, heat olive oil over medium-low heat. Cook the garlic until lightly browned. Add sage or thyme, drained beans, tomatoes, and broth. Cook over medium heat for 5 minutes. Season with salt and pepper to taste. Garnish with chopped parsley.
Serve with toasted peasant bread or on top of penne pasta. Serves 6 (per serving: 248 calories, 38g carbs, 13g protein, 5g fat, no cholesterol).
